entry.title: Explore SAP Build Low-Code at TechEd 2024
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is right across the corner and the spotlight shines brighter than ever on SAP Build—a comprehensive suite of low-code and pro-code tools designed to accelerate application development and automation with the power of generative AI. Whether you're an experienced developer or just beginning your digital journey, SAP Build is here to provide business you with the tools needed to simplify app creation, automate processes, and design digital workspaces.

This blog will focus on SAP Build’s low-code offerings at TechEd 2024, highlighting key sessions that showcase how low-code can empower you to drive innovation and transformation within your organization.

SAP TechEd Experience 2024 

SAP TechEd 2024 kicks off with an exciting, free virtual event that’s open to everyone, offering a truly engaging experience no matter where you are. It is a great chance to connect with industry experts, developers, and IT leaders to discover innovative strategies that will elevate your career and drive your company’s success.

   📅 Save the date: October 8–9, 2024 

   🤝 Interactive, accessible, and virtual event

   🚀 Register now! 

Following this, SAP TechEd on Tour will bring deep dive, in-person learning experience to various locations worldwide. 

 

Highlight: SAP Build Low-Code at TechEd virtual 2024 

   🔷 AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions

Speakers: Thomas Volmering, Björn Schulz, Alexander Rother 

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 6:00 PM - 6:45 PM CEST

Explore a fresh approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build solutions and the ABAP Cloud development model work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, you can smoothly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs.

   🔷 AD102 | Unlock SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility with SAP Build

Speakers: Florian Buech, Karthik Thirunavukkarasan

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 11:00 AM - 11:25 AM CEST

Explore the latest capabilities in SAP Build solutions to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while keeping your core clean. The extensibility wizard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides direct access to various extension options both on stack and side by side on SAP Business Technology Platform. New remote function call connectivity in SAP Build enables clean core extension of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using tier-two BAPIs.

   🔷 AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build

Speakers: Tim Back, Anne Keller

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.

   🔷 AD108 | Enhance your business efficiency with SAP Build

Speakers: Yumnah Abbasi, Sruthi Chiramel

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:30 PM - 8:55 PM CEST

Find out how customers extend their processes with SAP Build low-code solutions across HR, procurement, supply chain, finance, and sustainability applications. Learn how you can use prebuilt content in SAP Build to accelerate and benefit from the latest features, such as guided experiences.

   🔷 AD109 | Experience the next level of enterprise automation with SAP

Speakers: Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan, Palak Garg

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 1:00 PM - 1:25 PM CEST

Explore the latest enhancements using SAP Build solutions, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio solutions together. Drive your AI-powered business transformation by combining process analysis, process automation, and integration capabilities. Extend your approval processes with API-based integrations, and find out how you can leverage event-driven architectures, turn process insights and modelling outcomes into actions, and orchestrate your processes at scale in heterogenous landscapes.

   🔷 AD110 | Accelerate development with the latest AI capabilities in SAP Build

Speakers: Rupam Bhattacharya, Marc Huber

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:00 PM - 8:25 PM CEST

Discover the power of Joule in SAP Build solutions to accelerate your development process and AI features in SAP build that improve productivity of its users. Dive into the latest AI features designed for developers, enabling you to extend SAP S/4 HANA. Experience Gen AI powered fusion development firsthand in SAP Build. Learn how to automate processes and work flow productively with AI capabilities. Get a glimpse of what’s new and latest in AI features within SAP Build.

   🔷 AD181 | Develop applications and processes with SAP Build and Joule (Jump-Start session) 

Speakers: Eric Solberg, Patrick Schad

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 9:00 PM - 9:25 PM CEST

Immerse yourself in creating comprehensive intelligent applications using SAP Build solutions in this hands-on session. Gain comprehensive knowledge of full-stack development, process automation, and business site creation with SAP Build. Learn how SAP Cloud Application Programming Model is leveraged in a highly productive development environment with Joule technology.

   🔷 AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Speakers: Maria Kondratyeva, Miguel Angel Meza Martinez

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:00 PM - 8:25 PM CEST

Discover the powerful synergy between Joule technology and SAP Build solutions that facilitates a smooth extension-building process. Learn how to enhance AI-powered capabilities in Joule with custom-developed enterprise skills tailored to your company's specific needs. By integrating with both SAP and third-party applications, Joule can instantly apply its newfound knowledge within SAP systems. 

----------------------------------------

In addition to SAP Build highlights, SAP TechEd virtual offers a wide range of sessions, organized into six content tracks. These tracks cover the latest SAP technologies, products, and solutions, ensuring that you can dive deep into the topics most relevant to your role and industry.

   🔍 Browse the full session catalog!

In the Application Development and Automation track, you can dive into the latest innovations in SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Code, SAPUI5, Enterprise Automation with SAP, and more. The track offers practical demos and insights from our top speakers and product experts. 

   👩‍💻 Check out this blogpost for further details!

----------------------------------------

I hope this sneak peek has sparked your excitement to be part of SAP TechEd 2024 and explore all the latest updates around SAP Build and beyond. 

We can’t wait to see you there – Happy TechEd! 😊

entry.title: Enhancing Business Efficiency with SAP Build: Work Zone, Apps, and Process Automation
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) provides a robust suite of tools to help organizations build, extend, and enhance their digital capabilities. Among these tools are SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation. Each tool serves a unique purpose, from creating digital workplaces to developing custom applications and automating processes. This blog introduces these tools, outlines their key features, and provides examples to help you determine when to use each.

Intended Audience

This blog is intended for SAP developers, IT professionals, business process owners, and decision-makers looking to optimize digital experiences, streamline workflows, and accelerate digital transformation. Whether you are a developer seeking low-code solutions, an IT manager focusing on automation, or a business leader aiming to enhance employee productivity, this blog will help you understand how SAP Build tools can drive value for your organization.

Understanding the SAP Build Umbrella

SAP Build is a unified suite within SAP BTP that encompasses SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation. Together, these tools empower both business users and developers to design intuitive user interfaces, create custom applications, and automate complex workflows, all without the need for extensive coding skills.

  • SAP Build Work Zone focuses on creating a personalized digital workspace, where employees can access all their tools and information in one place.
  • SAP Build Apps is a low-code/no-code platform that enables rapid development of custom applications.
  • SAP Build Process Automation allows users to automate repetitive tasks and streamline business processes efficiently.

SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Work Zone enables organizations to create a unified digital workspace that enhances employee experience by integrating multiple applications, data sources, and services into a single, customizable portal. It is ideal for companies aiming to improve employee productivity and engagement by providing a personalized and consolidated workspace.

  • Key Features:
  • Unified access to SAP and third-party apps
  • Customizable user interfaces with drag-and-drop functionality
  • Pre-built templates for quick deployment
  • Role-based content delivery

Example Use Case: Create a central portal for HR that consolidates employee self-service tools, payroll information, training resources, and internal communication platforms.

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps is a low-code/no-code platform that allows users to rapidly develop and deploy custom applications. Designed for both business users and developers, it offers a visual development environment to build responsive applications that can connect to SAP and non-SAP data sources.

  • Key Features:
  • Low-code/no-code application development
  • Drag-and-drop interface for rapid prototyping
  • Pre-built connectors for easy integration with various data sources
  • Flexibility to customize app logic and design

Example Use Case: Develop a mobile application for field service agents to access real-time inventory data, log service requests, and update task statuses without needing extensive coding knowledge.

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Process Automation allows organizations to automate repetitive, manual tasks and streamline business processes using SAP’s AI and machine learning capabilities. This tool is perfect for companies looking to reduce errors, improve efficiency, and lower operational costs.

  • Key Features:
  • Visual workflow design and automation
  • Integration with SAP and third-party systems
  • Pre-built bot templates and AI services
  • Monitoring and analytics for process optimization

Example Use Case: Automate the end-to-end invoice processing workflow, from data extraction to approval, reducing manual intervention and accelerating processing times.

Comparison of SAP Build Tools

 

Conclusion and Next Steps

SAP BTP offers a powerful set of tools — SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation — to help organizations enhance employee experiences, rapidly develop applications, and automate processes. Start by identifying key areas where these tools can provide the most value, and consider initiating a small pilot project to explore their potential benefits. In the next blog, we’ll discuss advanced integration techniques and best practices for maximizing your SAP BTP investments.

entry.title: SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition. SAML configuration.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In this blog, I will try to clarify the architecture of SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition (SAP WZAE) with a focus on the SAML authentication process. Many existing guides provide step-by-step instructions, but often lack explanation of the key concepts behind the process. This blog will aim to fill that gap and provide clarity on the roles of IAS, IPS, SAML, and the relationship between SAP WZAE and DWS. Why SAML is emphasized - the SAML setup and understanding are a key to successfully install SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition. 

Without a deeper understanding of key concepts such as IAS, IPS, tenants and subaccounts in BTP, SAML, SAP WorkZone app (frontend), SAP DWS (backend), JAM (sometimes SAP SuccessFactors and the SAP Cloud Foundry environment) — the process of installing and configuring the SAP WorkZone ecosystem can feel random and unnecessarily challenging

The following description provides the list of main components that are required for authentication in SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition application:

  • IAS (Identity Authentication Service): A cloud-based service that handles authentication for users in SAP systems, enabling Single Sign-On (SSO) and secure user access using standards like SAML.

  • IPS (Identity Provisioning Service): A service for automating user provisioning, synchronizing users, roles, and permissions between different identity providers and target systems.

  • SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language): A protocol used for Single Sign-On (SSO) that allows identity providers (IdP) to authenticate users and authorize access to service providers (SP) securely.

  • SAP WorkZone App (Frontend): The user interface of SAP WorkZone, where users interact with applications, content, and workflows to manage their work and access business applications.

  • SAP DWS (Digital Workplace Service): The backend component of SAP WorkZone that manages user data, workspace configurations, and content through APIs, providing administrative functions and integration capabilities.

Please note, this guide will not provide another step-by-step installation guide for SAP WZAE. Instead, it aims to complement the existing blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-onboarding-series-step-by-step-guide-to-activate-your-sap-build/ba-p/13555072, which already provides detailed instructions on setting up SAP WZAE (in my opinion a few steps are not needed, but we will cover it later).

SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition (WZAE) and BTP

In simple terms, the BTP platform is gradually replacing SAP NetWeaver as the core platform for modern SAP applications. Just like SAP NetWeaver, BTP's kernel should be supported by the SAP Basis or another technical department in my view, as it involves many configurations and settings typically managed by SAP Basis teams in the NetWeaver environment.

To successfully configure and maintain a reliable and well-documented SAP WorkZone application or any other application in BTP, it's crucial to understand the architecture of SAP BTP, like we used to work with the architecture of classic SAP NetWeaver that includes few Application Servers, Database (single or cluster), and Web Dispatcher (optional), SSO and SAML configuration! The deeper understanding of the BTP backend ensures that the application setup is both effective and stable.

My recommendation for project associated with BTP - the team must have a consultant with technical background (SAP Platform support), otherwise you are in the risk to start the ping pong with SAP Support during implementation and finally will need to attract externals to finish setup.

Example of the SAP BTP Environment

This is a very basic diagram illustrating the different levels of the BTP environment. The key on this diagram is SAP Cloud Identity Service. The idea is to show that you don’t need to have the individual instance of SAP CIS inside of each Subaccount. It’s like a CUA system, usually there are two instances for all landscape - one for all production environments and another one for non productions.

When setting up SAP WorkZone, it is crucial for an administrator to have a solid understanding of the SAML protocol and how it is managed (IAS or IdP is installed inside of Cloud Identity Service that can work like standalone or proxy app. for local IdP [for instance MS Active Directory]). Knowing the concepts of the Identity Provider (IdP) and Service Provider (SP) — which are key components of SAML— is vital for ensuring a successful setup.

Key Components of SAP WorkZone

Before switch to technical aspects, it is important to note that SAP Work Zone application contains two key components:

  1. Front End component – SAP Build WorkZone advanced edition (SaaS)
  2. Backend component – Digital Workplace Service (DWS). Particularly, this instance stores user settings and authorization

SAML Configuration for SAP WZAE

Now the SAML, I would like to pay an attention on it because this is a key step in the configuration of SAP WZAE. The rest of steps that you can find in the official guide or blog are not so difficult:

  • official guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-advanced-edition/sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition/prerequisites
  • blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-onboarding-series-step-by-step-guide-to-activate-your-sap-build/ba-p/13555072

Authentication process in SAP Work Zone:

Authentication Process in SAP Work Zone

Let’s breakdown the above diagram:

  1. To be able to login in the Work Zone, the user must be created in IAS:

1.1 Additionally, the trust relationship must be established between subaccount of SAP WZAE and SAP IAS:

1.2 “Available for User Logon” option must be selected in the configuration of IAS:

1.3 The default identity provider should be disabled to prevent the usage of S-USER for connection:

2. SAP WZAE sends SAML request to IAS during login:

3. IAS sends back the SAML response with details about user:

3.1 Please pay an attention on AttributeStatement area, this is quite important. To provide a proper response the attributes of SAP WZAE application in IAS must be configured properly:

The saml trace can be obtained with SAML-tracer extension that is available in chrome:

Just turn it on before open the login page

4. Here the SAP magic starts - to be able to connect SAP WZAE app it makes the request to DWS. SAP WZAE calls the internal API /dynamic_dest/JAM/v1/single_use_tokens to generate the token and if it is ok, the response contains the location of DWS:

Our experience - we had the issue at this stage, this step likely failed because the subject name attributes were not correctly configured for the WorkZone application in IAS. However, since the URL included the "JAM" prefix, I initially thought we had missed something with the JAM integration, as JAM is mentioned multiple times in the official documentation:

As we later realized, JAM integration is not required, and there is no need for a connection with JAM at all (honestly, I still don’t know how the integration with JAM works, and I don’t intend to find out until this is mandatory for setup). The fact remains that the connection to JAM is not necessary to complete the setup of SAP WorkZone and /dynamic_dest/JAM/v1/single_use_tokens is not associated with JAM although JAM is presented in the URI.

The need to set up a SAML application for DWS is not well explained and can actually complicate the setup and troubleshooting process. Initially, I followed the instructions provided in this blog and created the application in IAS as suggested. However, after further investigation, I ended up deleting this application because I couldn’t find a valid reason to keep it. It didn’t seem to play a necessary role in the overall setup of SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition.

For your reference, the details about DWS can be obtained by using site manager configuration https://<the name of you wz_app>.dt.workzone.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com:

5. In accordance with diagram SAP WorkZone connects to DWS with ticket and here the IPS begins playing. So to that moment everything is good and you are sure you have the user that belongs to the one of the SAP Work Zone group:

But as we found this is not enough. To complete a user setup, IAS data must be replicated to the DWS and the bridge between SAP WZAE DWS and IAS is IPS 😊. In accordance with blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-onboarding-series-step-by-step-guide-to-activate-your-sap-build/ba-p/13555072 you have to create the source and target system in IPS and run the job to replicate the data. It's important, if you want to provide the access for new user, after user creation in IAS the resynch job must be executed in IPS to replicate data in DWS!

Why we need to create the SAML configuration for DWS in IAS I did not find. Finally, I deleted the SAML for DWS because it is not using.

How to find the location of IAS and IPS: https://iamtenants.accounts.cloud.sap/

I hope an explained 6 steps will give you a little more understanding of the backend of SAP Work Zone. A detailed setup guide is not included here for the following reasons:

  1. A detailed and accurate guide has already been provided in the blog by Nagesh Caparthy, which I recommend referring to for step-by-step instructions on setting up SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition.
  2. I hope that SAP will release a comprehensive guide for BTP similar to BC360. Without this, navigating the environment can feel like working blind. Ideally, we could benefit from documentation that is as clear and structured as the Integration & Exception Monitoring - Setup & Configuration guide, which is simple, user-friendly, and effective. For reference, you can find that guide here.
  3. If you encounter issues during the SAP WorkZone setup or with any other application involving SAML, feel free to share your experiences or ask questions in the comments below. I am happy to help!

To conclude, the key to successfully setting up SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition lies in understanding its architecture and the role of SAML for authentication. While existing step-by-step guides are useful, a deeper understanding of concepts like IAS, IPS, and DWS ensures smoother implementation. I hope this article provides clarity, and if you encounter issues, feel free to share your experiences or ask questions in the comments

entry.title: Streamlining Parental Leave Management with SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM)
entry.content:
Introduction

Managing parental leave can be a complex and sensitive process for organizations, requiring careful attention to compliance, communication, and record-keeping. SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM) offers robust tools to streamline and simplify the management of parental leave, ensuring that both employees and HR departments benefit from an efficient, transparent, and compliant process.

What is SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM)?

SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM) is a comprehensive suite designed to enhance service delivery and streamline business processes across various organizational functions. With its focus on automation, integration, and user-centric design, SAP ESM is ideal for managing complex workflows and ensuring adherence to organizational policies and legal requirements.

Reference Blogs

  • New features - SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM) 
  • SAP Enterprise Service Management 

The below story revolves around the above features mentioned in blog

Initiate Self-Service 

Prasad an employee of Prasad Industries, needed to apply for parental leave. He swiftly accessed the parental leave section via the Initiate Self-Service feature in the SAP Work Zone, his centralized digital workplace. This included selecting the type of leave he was requesting and attaching supporting documents, such as a medical certificate. The process was smooth, and she appreciated how straightforward it was to submit everything online.

 

Access Notification to Open Case

As soon as Prasad submitted her case, the HR team received an Access Notification alerting them that a new parental leave case had been opened. Sarah, the HR manager, quickly reviewed the notification in her SAP ESM dashboard.

AI-Generated Case Summary

The system also created an AI-Generated Case Summary, giving Sarah a concise overview of Prasad's request, including key details and the supporting documents she had uploaded. This summary helped Sarah quickly understand the nature of the case without needing to sift through multiple forms. Sarah then proceeded with the Case Form Examination. She reviewed Prasad's submitted details, verifying that everything was in order and that no critical information was missing. The thorough examination ensured that the case could move forward without unnecessary delays.

Employee Signature Request via DocuSign

With everything in place, the next step was to secure Prasad's acknowledgment. Sarah initiated an Employee Signature Request via DocuSign, a seamless integration within the SAP ESM system. Prasad received the request to sign off on the leave agreement document.

Open Document from Email

Sarah was notified via email and opened the document directly from her inbox. The ease of access meant she could quickly review the terms of her leave and sign the document without needing to log back into the ESM portal. Prasad **Signed and Completed the Document** using DocuSign. The system immediately updated the case status to reflect the completion of this step, triggering the next phase in the workflow.

Signed Document Examination

Sarah received a notification that the document had been signed. She performed a **Signed Document Examination** to ensure everything was in order, confirming that all terms were agreed upon and legally binding.

The case was now fully resolved, ready to be archived.

Conclusion

In this seamless, automated process powered by SAP ESM, Prasad's parental leave case was handled efficiently and transparently. From initiation to completion, every step was streamlined, ensuring both the employee and HR team could navigate the process with ease. The integration of AI and automation into SAP ESM not only saved time but also ensured that the entire process was compliant with company policies and legal requirements. This successful resolution is just one example of how SAP ESM is transforming HR operations, making them more efficient, reliable, and user-friendly.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling (SAP Build) Sessions
entry.content:
Today is the first day of Devoberfest!!

Make sure you partake of the:

  • Contest -- grand prize is a trip to an SAP TechEd event with a lot of extras; and even though it's several years' old I still love the gameboard
  • Petoberfest – post a picture of your pet 🐢
  • Sessions – find all the sessions in the event calendar 
  • 👉 The annual SAP Crossword Puzzle – if you have the skill, you can earn 1800 points!

Devtoberfest 2024 sessions

Here's a quick rundown of the SAP Build "Tooling Tuesday" sessions, which touch on the following topics:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone
  • SAP Build Code
  • MDK
  • S/4HANA + extensions
  • Integration Suite, including event mesh
  • CAP
  • AI Core
  • GenAI + Joule
  • SAP Cloud Transport Management

That's just one day of Devtoberfest!! In each session, you'll have a chance to ask questions directly to the presenter, who will answer them live or in the chat.

September 24

  • 🟣 Transform Data with Scripting in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application with SAP Build Code

  • 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro

  • 🟣 Real-World S/4HANA Invoice Approval Process with SAP Build Process Automation

October 1

  • 🟣 SAP Build Apps 2049: Soon with UI5 and GenAI?

  • 🟣 Enable Work Zone to Consume Business Solutions from Other BTP Subaccounts (content federation)

  • 🟣 Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK

  • 🟣 Putting It All Together: S/4HANA Retail Pricing App Using SAP Build, AI, Event Mesh and More

October 15

  • 🟣 S/4HANA Extensions: Start Fast with the New Extensibility Wizard

  • 🟣 Guided Experiences with SAP Build Process Automation and Work Zone

  • 🟣 Develop Even Faster (!) with SAP Build Apps Templates

  • 🟣 Boost Development with GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation

October 22

  • 🟣 Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps

  • 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects

  • 🟣 Leveraging Decisions and Business Rules in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Extend Joule with Your Own Skills

Learn a lot about SAP development tools, have a lot of fun – and maybe we'll see you at an SAP TechEd event next year 😸

entry.title: Enabling Email Notifications in SAP Build Work Zone via Microsoft 365 Exchange Online (OAuth-Based)
entry.content:
Email notifications are an essential component for organizations using SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Task Center and other products delivered by SAP to keep users informed about updates, events, and tasks, see a diagram below as a reference visualizing SAP Task Center use case about sending  email notifications.

Microsoft announced the deprecation of Basic Authentication for Exchange Online (as outlined here). It means users of Microsoft 365 must now switch to OAuth-based authentication for sending email notifications. This change impacts users of SAP Build Work Zone and other SAP cloud products like SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Task Center.Thus, to ensure email notifications continue functioning, users of Microsoft 365, must configure SAP BTP subaccount destination for email notifications in SAP Build Work Zone using OAuth Client Credentials Flow (applicable to Microsoft 365 Exchange Online).

Together with my team-mate Angel Tonchev, we have noticed that SAP customers experience challenges to setup Microsoft 365 Exchange Online to OAuth-based authentication and then use these properties to setup the correctly SMTP Mail Destination in SAP BTP. This is why, we joint forces to prepate this guide. I hope you will find it useful! 

Prerequisites

Before configuring the Internet mail destination with OAuth Client Credentials Flow in your SAP BTP subaccount, ensure that notifications for SAP Build Work Zone have already been enabled. Follow the instructions outlined in the official SAP documentation here to set up notifications from your cloud solutions.

Configuring OAuth-Based Authentication for Microsoft 365

To generate the needed OAuth-Based Authentication properties, these configurations are to be done in your Microsoft 365 account:

  1. Open “office.com”. Click on the dots in the upper left corner and choose “Admin”
  2. On the left panel choose “Show all”

     

    3. After that, choose “All admin centers” and from there choose “Microsoft Entra"

3.1. In the Microsoft Entra admin center, navigate to Identity -> Applications -> App registration and choose “New registration”

4. Enter a name and click Register

5. On next window, choose “Certificates and secrets” -> “New client secret”

Note down the Value as it is a one-time view:

6. Next, on the left-side menu  choose: “API permissions” and then "Add a permission"

7. From there, choose “APIs my organization uses” -> “Office 365 Exchange Online”

8. Then, choose “Application permissions”, search for “smtp” and select “SMTP.SendAsApp”. Choose “Add permissions”

9. On next window choose “Grant admin consent for MSFT”

10. App registration - Overview page:  note down Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID

Register a Microsoft Entra app and create a service principal

Once that’s done, you need to create a dedicated technical user email for sending alerts. You can decide which user is going to be used for sending emails or you can decide to create a new technical one for that.

1. From Microsoft Admin Center, navigate to: Users -> Add a user and follow the procedure as per the screenshot below.

Then, from Microsoft Admin Center, navigate to Users-> Active Users, and select the created / newly added user:
- click “Mail” under the user -> select “Manage email apps”
- make sure that “Authenticated SMTP” is checked.

Register the app service principle in Exchange Online with PowerShell or to the Azure environment (in case you have access). To do so , please check the documentation here: Register a Microsoft Entra app and create a service principal

Note: you can also consult the official documentation for PowerShell

In PowerShell:
1. Run line  “Install-Module -Name ExchangeOnlineManagement -allowprerelease” (on error remove “– allowprerelease”)
2. Run line  “Import-module ExchangeOnlineManagement”
3. Run line “Connect-ExchangeOnline -Organization <Directory (tenant) ID>”
NOTE: “Directory (tenant) ID” which we noted down in “Procedure” step #10
4. Run line “New-ServicePrincipal -AppId < Application (client) ID > -ObjectId <Object ID>”

NOTE: “Application (client) ID” which we noted down in “Procedure” step #10
 The Object ID is different than the one that we used in the one visible on APP creation. To get the right onе, go the “Microsoft Entra admin center”->“Identity“->“Applications“->“Enterprise applications“->“All Applications“. Select your app->from “Overview”, you can copy the Object ID

5.Run line “ Add-MailboxPermission -Identity "username@domain.com" -User  <Object ID> -AccessRights FullAccess”
NOTE: For “Identity” use the user created specially created in the “Create dedicated User” section

That’s an example on how the outcome should look like:

Configure a Destination service in your SAP BTP subaccount

Now as we are ready with all the needed configurations from Microsoft365 side, we proceed with applying the needed properties in your Destination service in your SAP BTP subaccount. To do so, please follow the steps listed underneath:

1. Access your SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) cockpit.

2. Go to the Destinations section under your subaccount and click “Create Destination”

3. Create a new destination for the SMTP Mail server with the properties described in this procedure, see point (b) Internet mail destination with OAuth Client Credentials Flow :

Example destination:

    • Name: SAP_Business_Notifications_Mail
    • Type: Mail
    • Proxy Type: Internet
    • Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
    • Client ID: <Application (client) ID ” see step #10 “App registration - Overview page”
    • Client Secret: <Secret ID>, see step #5: “Certificates and secrets” -> “New client secret”
    • Token Service URL Type: Dedicated
    • Token Service URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/<Tenant ID>/oauth2/v2.0/token
      Note: for <Tenant ID> use the “Directory (tenant) ID” from step #10 “App registration- Overview page”

 Additional Properties:

    • mail.smtp.host: smtp.office365.com
    • mail.smtp.port: 587
    • mail.username: <technical_user_email_address>
    • mail.smtp.from: <technical_user_email_address>
    • scope: https://outlook.office365.com/.default
    • [optional] mail.smtp.timeout: 5000

Testing Your Configuration

Once the configuration is complete, send a test email notification from SAP Build Work Zone to ensure everything is working as expected, see a test email I was able to receive shared below:.

Recap

With the deprecation of Basic Authentication in Microsoft 365 Exchange Online, it’s crucial for SAP Build Work Zone users to switch to OAuth-based authentication for SMTP Mail destinations. This guide has provided step-by-step instructions for configuring Microsoft 365 OAuth Client Credentials Flow, ensuring seamless email notifications for SAP cloud products.

This configuration not only applies to SAP Build Work Zone but can also be used for other SAP products like SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Task Center, enabling secure and reliable email notifications.

We hope this guide has been helpful. If you have any questions or suggestions, we’re open to your feedback! Feel free to share your thoughts in the comments.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Greetings, Builders! We’re pleased to bring you the Q3 Edition of our What’s New in SAP Build series. In this edition, we’re covering product-specific updates for:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Code

SAP Build Apps

Release management and project lifecycle management: It’s now possible to release and transport projects across different tenants using SAP Cloud Transport Management System. You can transport project versions between subaccounts while isolating development, testing, and productive deployment of SAP Build Apps applications to their own landscapes. To get started, see this blog post.

Performant table list with large dataset from S/4HANA: Managing and displaying large amounts of data in an application can be challenging and cause performance issues. Check out this example of creating a performant list page for an S/4HANA Cloud endpoint with 65k+ records - see the blog post.

Copying pages within one project: This feature enables users to copy and duplicate a page within a project in SAP Build Apps – a simple, yet powerful, add-on to further speed up development.

New Build Service: We recently launched a new and improved experience for deploying application packages. The build service includes managing image assets, iOS usage strings, mobile service enablement, package creation, and configuration management. The updated UI provides a much smoother experience for developers at the deployment phase, ensuring that every requirement is met and that your apps are ready to publish. Try it out today!

  • Also check out Ludo Noens’ series on Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps – Creating a CRUD Application
  • Learn more about what’s coming up on the SAP Build Apps Roadmap.

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud: The extensibility wizard is now available with the 2408 edition of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. This makes the creation of side-by-side extensions even more streamlined and allows you to seamlessly navigate to SAP Build from your Cloud ERP. This first release enables custom process creation, with more options from SAP Build to become available this year. See more in the blog.

Script Task Editor: It's now possible to write JavaScript code in the new Script Editor, allowing the execution of complex data type transformations and additional operations on the process context. Previously this was limited to automation - now it includes process inputs and custom variables, facilitating advanced data manipulation and transformation. See more in the blog post.

Scheduled trigger: Now you can schedule process automations based on user-defined time intervals and dates at runtime. This allows you to enable system-centric and time-based process automation use cases, such as working and nonworking days in creating your automation scenarios to avoid resource mismatches.

Additional Outcomes in Forms and UI5 Support for Tasks: This enables citizen developers to configure additional outcome actions in the process to let approvers request rework in case of missing or wrong information in approval forms. You can also start filling out multiple forms efficiently through a guided experience.

Advanced Event Project Creation with Manual Specification Upload: This allows users to upload business event specifications for the creation of an event project in the lobby. The new feature adds support for business events across lines of business (LOBs) and custom events, including third-party events that are supported at runtime using SAP Event Mesh.

Process Visibility enhancements: These include actionable insights, instance tracking for requestors and additional monitoring capabilities via CALM. Now you can turn insights into meaningful outcomes and enable the triggering of escalation flows based on visibility insights, to directly react and resolve critical situations. This enables auditable and governed escalation resolution, instead of having disconnected communication through different channels.

More on SAP Build Process Automation:

  • See the What's New Viewer for more updates, and improvements.
  • See SAP Road Map Explorer for more upcoming features and focus areas.

SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, and SAP Task Center

SAP Build Work Zone, both editions

HTML5 Business Solution as a Content Provider: With the latest update in SAP Build Work Zone, you can now expose BTP-based applications as Work Zone content, like roles, spaces, and pages, in the 'Common Data Model' (CDM) format. As a result, you can easily configure and enable access to BTP applications from a Work Zone instance, even across subaccounts. See more in the Help Portal and this blog post.

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Define Sort Order of pages and spaces: Administrators can now configure how spaces and pages are sorted on the menu bar of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. This feature is only applicable if you're working in the new experience view mode for spaces and pages. See more in the Help Portal.

Import and Export of Pages and Spaces: Content manager tools will be enhanced to enable administrators to export and import custom (locally created) spaces and pages between landscapes.

SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition

Integrated guided experience across SAP Build Work Zone and Process Automation: Guided processes are now available as an artifact in SAP Build Process Automation. In addition to the previously available “no template” wizards, it is now possible to create guided, process-based wizards in SAP Build Work Zone. See more information in the blog post.

SAP Start

Directly navigate to product home pages of integrated cloud solutions: The SAP Start site offers a new product section to easily see and navigate to the product home pages of the integrated solutions. This enables transparency of available cloud solutions for the current user, and quick and easy navigation to launch the product home page of the desired solution.

SAP Task Center

ITCE partner page: SAP Task Center now allows connections with third-party task providers. The first solution to leverage this capability is TaskConnect, developed by ITCE in collaboration with SAP architects and technical consultants. The application will soon be available on the SAP Store as well, offering a comprehensive configuration guide, installation manuals and BPMN diagrams. For more information about TaskConnect, see here.

More on SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start and SAP Task Center:

  • Check out the roadmaps for SAP Build Work Zone standard & advanced edition and SAP Start / SAP Task Center
  • What's New Viewers: Standard edition / Advanced edition / SAP Start / SAP Task Center 

SAP Build Code

New AI Features: In SAP Build Code, you can now use the Joule copilot, our generative AI assistant, to write code by providing natural language prompts. For a detailed explanation of Joule, please read this article. In the last months we added many new features to this capability including the following highlights:

  • Support SAP S/4HANA Cloud Service Integration for Data Model and App Logic: It is now possible to use Joule to generate code for your integrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud services.
  • Major UX Update of Joule in SAP Build Code: With the latest UX update of Joule within SAP Build Code we enhanced the usability, streamlined workflows, and created a more intuitive interface, boosting productivity and satisfaction.
  • Generative AI support for productivity tools: We now allow you to use Joule directly from the storyboard.
  • Use Joule to enhance your already existing data models and services: It is now possible to modify already existing data model and service entities using a prompt.

Why SAP Build Code: Check out this blog post by Product Manager Rupa Maity describing why to move to SAP Build Code.

Stay updated on the latest Build Code innovations: Do you want to stay informed about the latest product updates for SAP Build Code? Follow our tag on SAP Community and subscribe to our SAP Build Code Newsletter. 

  • See the latest update product updates from September 2024 for SAP Build Code.
  • Learn more on the SAP Build Code Community.

--- 

💡Please note this blog post provides a highlight of the latest features and enhancements. 

  • For comprehensive list of all updates, please refer to our Help Portal What’s New in SAP Build.
  • Join the SAP Builders group for product resources and community questions.
  • Devtoberfest is here! 🥨 Don't miss our SAP Build-themed sessions and more.
  • ...and that means SAP TechEd is right around the corner! Explore SAP Build at TechEd 2024.

Which feature are you most excited to test out? Let us know in the comments!

entry.title: 🚀Fire of Innovations: Highlights from SAP TechEd 2024 📢
entry.content:
The SAP TechEd 2024 event, themed “Fire of Innovations,” has once again set the stage for groundbreaking advancements in enterprise technology. Held virtually on October 8-9, this year’s conference showcased a plethora of new tools, updates, and strategies designed to empower businesses and developers alike. Here’s a deep dive into some of the most exciting announcements and innovations from the event.

1. Joule: The AI Copilot Revolution

One of the standout highlights was the unveiling of new capabilities for Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot. Joule has been supercharged with collaborative AI agents that can perform complex, cross-disciplinary tasks. These agents are designed to break down silos within organizations, enhancing productivity and allowing human workers to focus on more creative and strategic activities.

For instance, in dispute management, autonomous AI agents can now analyze and resolve issues such as incorrect invoices or duplicate payments. In financial accounting, these agents streamline processes like bill payments and ledger updates, quickly addressing any inconsistencies.

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Enhancements

SAP continues to expand its Business Technology Platform (BTP), which is central to its cloud strategy. This year, SAP announced the expansion of BTP coverage through eight new SAP-managed data centers. Additionally, the SAP Discovery Center now offers a Quick-Account-Setup feature, enabling businesses to deploy specific use cases with just a click.

The integration of ABAP Cloud into SAP Build was another significant update. This integration allows developers to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby, ensuring a seamless and efficient development process.

3. Innovations in SAP Build

SAP Build has been strengthened to support a “Clean Core” strategy, which is crucial for maintaining system consistency and upgradeability. The SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, introduced for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, helps both key users and developers create on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

4. Harnessing the Power of Data with SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP introduced the SAP Knowledge Graph, a next-generation solution that connects SAP data with rich business context. This tool is poised to help developers unlock the full value of SAP data, driving more informed decision-making and innovation.

5. Global Reach and Accessibility

This year’s SAP TechEd was a truly global event, with a free two-day virtual format that included keynotes, technical sessions, and developer enablement activities. The event was designed to be accessible to a worldwide audience, ensuring that everyone could benefit from the latest SAP innovations.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 has once again demonstrated SAP’s commitment to driving innovation and empowering businesses with cutting-edge technology. From AI advancements with Joule to enhancements in SAP BTP and SAP Build, the “Fire of Innovations” theme truly encapsulates the transformative potential of these new tools and strategies. As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of the digital age, SAP’s innovations provide a robust foundation for growth and success.

Stay tuned for more updates and insights from SAP TechEd as we continue to explore the future of enterprise technology.

Feel free to share your thoughts or ask any questions about the event! What innovation are you most excited about?

entry.title: Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build
entry.content:
Table of Content

  • Motivation for Guided Experiences

  • New Guided Process Modelling: Advanced Automation and Flexibility

  • Connecting Guided Processes as Wizard Templates in SAP Build Work Zone

  • Conclusion – SAP Build Guided Experiences

  • What's Next?

  • Additional Read

Motivation for Guided Experiences

Guided experiences are designed to simplify multi-step business processes by breaking them down into manageable and intuitive steps. Each step can be enriched with various types of content, including videos, documents, forms, tasks, UI cards, and apps to provide users with all the necessary information to complete each step. Steps can be classified as optional or mandatory, ensuring critical tasks are completed while allowing flexibility for other actions. Users don't need to worry about finishing all the steps at once as the progress is saved, enabling them to resume from where they left off. Guided experiences can be used for various complex processes such as parental leave, benefits enrolment, employee relocation, new hires onboarding or suppliers onboarding, training and enablement, and browsing and purchasing electronic equipment. To learn more about the background & motivation around guided experiences, please see our previous blog about this capability.

New Guided Process Modelling: Advanced Automation and Flexibility

The major enhancement in the October 2024 release is the ability to model guided processes directly in SAP Build Process Automation, bringing powerful process automation to your SAP Build projects. The following key aspects have been added:

Guided Process Artefact

The new ‘Guided Process’ artefact allows you to design and manage structured, multi-step processes within SAP Build Process Automation. This feature is perfect for creating more dynamic and reusable workflows.

Stages and Steps 

Following the structure introduced for the wizard layout, ‘Guided Processes’ equally allow to create stages & steps within each stage.  Additionally, these process steps can be designed to be sequential or parallel flows, allowing for both straightforward and complex workflows.

Create a StageAdd a Step to a Stage

 

Note: with this release, only SAP Build Work Zone is supported as ‘Step UI’; meaning the step itself needs to designed via the Work Zone wizard layout editor (for example to add UI integration cards).

Advanced Logic Integration

To create more sophisticated processes as basis for your guided experience, branches can be used to structure the overall guided process and duration checks can be incorporated via wait for duration to handle conditional starting of stages. Furthermore, subprocesses can be used to handle more complex requirements, such as retrieving hire date information.

Easy Deployment to SAP Build Work Zone

Once the guided process is modelled, it can be deployed to SAP Build Work Zone as a wizard layout template, ensuring easy availability for end-users. This provides a seamless transition from process design to execution.

Note: the deployment to SAP Build Work Zone is performed on behalf of the logged-in user via the automatically created destination pointing to DWS as per the following guide.

Shared Environments in SAP Build

This new ‘Guided Process’ artefact is only supported in shared environments, not in the default / public one. To learn more about shared environments in SAP Build, please refer to the following blog post.

Connecting Guided Processes as Wizard Templates in SAP Build Work Zone

In addition to modelling ‘wizard layouts’ directly in SAP Build Work Zone (which is now available via the “New Wizard” option)...

Add Wizard LayoutSelect Process for WizardOption for Standalone Wizard

 

… it is now possible to select ‘Guided Processes’ modelled in SAP Build Process Automation as the underlying structure when creating guided experiences.

Guided Process-based Wizards

Users can now select one of previously deployed guided processes they have access to. These serve as the template the wizard layout within SAP Build Work Zone.

Note: While modelling & deploying guided processes via the SAP Build Lobby requires access to the ‘advanced user’ license in SAP Build Process Automation, that is not the case for consuming deployed processes. For doing so, the user requires the following permissions:

  • Workpage edit permissions in Work Zone to edit the workpage, add wizard layouts,
  • ProcessAutomationParticipant role collection
  • 'Execute' permission for shared environment (Share an Environment | SAP Help Portal)

Note: creating wizard layouts based on ‘guided processes’ is currently released in preview environments of SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition / SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone and will become available in the non-preview environments with the subsequent release.

Auto-generated Wizard Structure

When selecting a guided process, the wizard layout is automatically generated based on the process definition, saving time and ensuring consistency.

Design Wizard with Widgets & UI Cards

Just like with a manually-created one, you can customize guided process-based wizard layouts using different widgets and UI cards, providing flexibility in designing the user experience.

  • Adding Widgets / Cards to each step: 
  • Example Step with Image widget:

Note: Already in a previous release, UI cards have been enhanced to interact with wizard layouts in SAP Build Work Zone, for instance to enforce users taking certain actions. Details and code examples for such UI cards are available via our central SAP Build Work Zone GitHub sample repository.

Process Instance Creation

When a user starts a wizard based on a guided process, an instance of that process is started automatically, allowing execution & resuming of the steps according to the defined process structure.

Conclusion – SAP Build Guided Experiences

This streamlined integration reduces development time and ensures that teams can leverage the power of SAP Build Process Automation alongside SAP Build Work Zone bringing exciting possibilities for creating powerful & content-rich guided experiences. These innovations not only simplify process design and execution but also ensure that you can deploy efficient, scalable guided experiences tailored to your unique needs – bringing different capabilities from SAP Build together via a deeply integrated, end-to-end experience!

What’s Next?

Looking ahead, we are planning to introduce additional features to further enhance guided experiences. These upcoming capabilities aim to add additional flexibility for creating, designing & operating guided experiences with SAP Build.  Here's a sneak peek at what's planned (*):

Spoiler
  • Repeating Guided Experiences
  • Starting Multiple Instances of the same Guided Experience
  • Support for more Step UIs, including Forms
  • Integration with Process Visibility for Monitoring
  • […]

In addition to those additional enhancements, we are also working on providing additional pre-built business content (packages) for guided experiences (*), such as onboarding in context of SAP SuccessFactors. Stay tuned as these new features roll out, offering even more ways to optimize your guided experiences and streamline your business processes!

(*) Disclaimer: Please note that the roadmap items shared here are subject to change and may not be delivered as initially planned. The information provided is for informational purposes only and does not constitute a commitment by SAP. As always, the best resource to learn about upcoming capabilities is the central roadmap explorer.

Additional Read

To learn more about the topic covered in this blog post, please also refer to the following assets & follow us on SAP community.

  • Related Blog Posts:
    • What’s new with SAP Build Work Zone guided experiences
    • SAP Build Process Automation - Introducing Environments
  • Learning Journeys & Certification: 
    • Work Zone: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning
    • Process Automation: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Reference Architectures:
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-sap-build-work-zone
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-build-process-automation
  • SAP Help guides for more details:
    • Work Zone Preview: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-advanced-edition/sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition/what... (will move to regular admin guide with next release)
    • Process Automation: Create a Guided Process
  • GitHub Repository for Work Zone Examples: https://github.com/SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration/tree/main/advanced/wizard

entry.title: Your guide to Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build - Frequently asked questions
entry.content:
Welcome everyone! 

In this blogpost, I will breakdown the most frequently asked questions about guided experiences, an innovative capability powered by SAP Build. I will address questions around how it works, the benefits it offers, common use cases and much more. Let’s get started on unlocking the full potential of guided experiences. 

🔐For those looking to dive deeper into the capability, I recommend exploring our expert blogpost, where you'll find a comprehensive breakdown of its features. 

***

✔️ What are guided experiences in SAP Build?

Guided experience is a capability within SAP Build, a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This capability enables business users to simplify complex business processes into self-service journeys that are easy to follow and execute. It allows users to create interactive, step-by-step guides for navigating and using applications.

✔️ What are the key aspects of guided experiences?

Key aspects of guided experiences are as below. 

  • Step-by-step guidance: Users are guided through a series of steps to complete a specific task or process performed within SAP Build Work Zone with use cases often spanning across multiple systems. 
  • Customizable content: Users can create custom content for the guided experiences, including text, images, and videos, to provide clear and concise instructions, with no- and low-code capabilities.
  • Interactive elements: Guided experiences can include elements like UI Cards and default widgets (image, text, content, etc.) to engage users and ensure smooth execution.

✔️ What are some common use cases for guided experiences?

Some use cases examples can be: 

  • Creating one simple employee onboarding journey for new employees
  • Creating step-based guide to deliver best practices, training and relevant material to empower sales teams
  • Simplified purchase order verification process while purchasing equipment
  • Creating a smooth onboarding journey for new suppliers
  • Seamless journey for processing invoices across channel partners
  • And many more...

✔️Which SAP Build solutions are included in guided experiences?

Guided experiences incorporate capabilities from all the low-code SAP Build solutions - SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps.

✔️ How does guided experiences drive business value?

  • Enhanced User Experience: Empowers workforces with personalized digital experiences, boosting satisfaction, engagement, and retention.
  • Increased Productivity: Automates tasks and streamlines processes, enabling employees to focus on high-impact work.
  • Greater Agility: Rapidly deploys, modifies, and scale digital capabilities with intuitive tools, empowering business builders without IT dependency

✔️What is a 'wizard layout' in guided experiences?

Wizard layout the actual user interface in SAP Build Work Zone that the end-user work with. It allows users to create a step-by-step experience on any workpage. This layout can consist of one or multiple stages, each containing various steps. These steps or stages can be set as optional or mandatory, depending on the process requirements. Users can incorporate different UI elements available in Work Zone, such as tiles, widgets, UI cards, and other applications into each step.

✔️ What is a 'guided process' in guided experiences?

For more advanced processes, it is possible to create a ‘guided process’ as an artefact in SAP Build Process Automation and make use of the powerful capabilities of Process Automation. The guided process can then be deployed to SAP Build Work Zone and then used as the foundation/template when creating a wizard layout as an alternative option to creating the wizard manually directly in Work Zone.

✔️ What authorization is required for a user to create a Guided Experience in SAP Build Work Zone using a predefined template?

The user must have end-user permission/authorization. Any user with the permission to create and edit a work page can modify and create wizards within SAP Build Work Zone. When creating a guided experience wizard layout based on a ‘guided process’ template, the user requires access to a deployed template in either the public or another environment in Process Automation. To model and deploy the ‘guided process’ artefact, the advanced user permission is required in Process Automation side.

✔️ Can a user save their progress in SAP Build Work Zone for gradual competition?

Yes, progress of the user within the wizard layout is saved automatically so that they return and complete the steps another time – if needed.

✔️ Does the user mark completion of their own tasks or does Work Zone pick up on the status from other systems?

Work Zone has the ability to verify if a specific activity has been completed by the user. For mandatory steps, Work Zone ensures that the task is completed before allowing progress in the process. In each step, tasks / activities from other business systems could be incorporated via UI5 cards (where the task would then be completed). The UI card framework has also been enhanced to support interaction with the wizard layout to check the completion of a task. See here!

✔️ Are guided experiences available in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition?

No, they are not available in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and there are currently no plans to enable this capability in standard edition either.

✔️ Are guided experiences available in SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone?

 Yes, additionally to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, they are also available in SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. 

✔️ Given the visibility scenarios in SAP Build Process Automation, what additional benefits does SAP Build Work Zone offer?

SAP Build Work Zone provides the consumption layer and easy wizard editor that can be conveniently redefined by content administrator at runtime. With SAP Build Process Automation ‘guided process’ as the process backbone for the wizard layout, the flexible design & content capabilities of Work Zone provide the user-facing capabilities. Additionally, aspects like UI5 cards enable flexible, context-rich integration of business data across SAP and 3rd party solutions in each individual wizard step.

✔️ Is it possible to for the end-user to restart the completed process/step?

Capabilities to restart a completed process are on the roadmap, which can then be enabled in the guided experience configuration. Currently a guided experience can enforce a restart when editing and publishing a new version; alternatively, it can be configured to allow existing processes to complete with the previous version until completion.

✔️ What guided experience capabilities are on the roadmap?

Looking ahead into the H2 2024 and beyond, many additional capabilities are planned for the SAP Build guided experience capability. For instance integration with process visibility, support for additional step UIs like forms, ability for users to start multiple guided experiences for the same template and more.

Further to this, additional business content examples – for example onboarding from SAP SuccessFactors – are being created to get started more easily. This business content can then be used as template and tweaked as required.

***

I hope this blogpost has addressed your questions. If you want to learn more, feel free to explore the additional resources listed below.

➤ SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community

➤ SAP Build Process Automation on: sap.com / SAP Community 

➤ SAP Builders Community Group 

➤ Learning Journey: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning

➤ Learning Journey: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

➤ SAP TechEd session ft. guided experiences: (AD108) Enhance Business Efficiency with SAP Build 

➤ SAP Devtoberfest session: Guided Experiences with SAP Build

entry.title: Announcing the New "In-Metro" Disaster Recovery Solution for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Overview

We’re excited to unveil the latest enhancement to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP):
The In-Metro Disaster Recovery (DR) solution.

This solution is designed to protect you against disasters affecting a single Availability Zone (AZ) with contractually committed SLAs RPO and RTO, offering enhanced resilience and uninterrupted operations for your cloud services.

 

What is "In-Metro" DR for SAP BTP?

The In-Metro DR solution is built to protect against local disasters by leveraging a Multi-AZ setup. It ensures that, in the event of a single AZ disaster, which is defined by SAP, services remain operational, data is protected, and business continues without significant downtime.

The key metrics are:

  • RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 5 minutes – Ensuring that data can be recovered with a maximum of 5 minutes' loss in case of a disaster.
  • RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 2 hours – Ensuring that the full system is restored and operational within 2 hours of an incident.

 

The Power of Multi-AZ Architecture

At the heart of In-Metro DR is the Multi-AZ (Multi Availability Zone) architecture, ensuring that your applications and databases are spread across different zones to safeguard against single-point failures.

This includes:

  • Active-active applications across 3 AZs: Applications are distributed across three AZs, with real-time data replication ensuring that if one AZ fails, the others automatically take over without impacting availability.
  • Active-passive databases across 2 AZs: Databases are set up in active-passive mode, with a failover mechanism in place to switch operations to a backup AZ if the primary one fails.

Through automatic failover, load balancing, and failure detection, the solution ensures that services stay online seamlessly. Internal stress tests, such as regular “Chaos Day” exercises, further reinforce the reliability of this DR solution.

 

Availability and Scope

 As of now, the In-Metro DR solution is not available for all SAP BTP services. While it already covers a wide range of services, its scope will continue to expand over time as we improve coverage and extend it to additional SAP BTP services. The services in scope will be regularly updated in the corresponding documents.

In-Metro DR is specifically designed for SAP-managed scenarios, meaning that customers using SAP BTP under SAP’s management can take advantage of the In-Metro DR setup without additional configuration or costs. However, for customer-managed scenarios, such as side-by-side extensions, the customer is responsible for configuring it accordingly (e.g. deploy the apps with multiple instances or configure data replicas on the persistency level).

 

 DR Planning and Regular Testing

 To ensure the highest levels of reliability:

  • The DR plan is updated every 12 months, ensuring that it incorporates the latest technology and best practices.
  • A DR test is conducted at least once a year to verify that the solution can meet its RPO and RTO commitments under real-world conditions.

The In-Metro DR solution currently supports SAP BTP regions hosted on both AWS and Azure cloud infrastructures, providing flexibility for your organization’s cloud setup.

 

 Next Steps

 By leveraging In-Metro DR within SAP-managed scenarios, you can trust that your critical data and services are protected with cutting-edge resilience, without the need for extra investment. For customer-managed scenarios, setting up corresponding HA and DR solutions will ensure your setup is equally resilient.

To explore which services are already covered and get all information, check the description in SAP Trust Center.

Alternatively, you can watch a deep dive into this new solution at SAP TechED: Watch the SAP TechED session on In-Metro DR.

entry.title: SAP is a Visionary in 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms
entry.content:
Today, I am very proud to share the great news: Gartner has recognized SAP as a Visionary in its 2024 Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms (LCAP).

Let me share with you how we got here, what we believe makes us a Visionary in low-code application development with SAP Build and how you can get started with SAP’s one-stop-shop for extensibility and automations.

SAP Build – the low-code platform for SAP customers

We have designed SAP Build as the best software suite for SAP customers that want to efficiently extend their SAP solutions: SAP Build fosters fusion development allowing active collaboration between business users and professional developers, it brings Business AI with Joule copilot to boost development productivity, prebuilt LoB solutions with context-based recommendations, and deep integration with business applications.

A bit of history

Two years ago, at SAP TechEd 2022, we announced the launch of a powerful new offering to drive the next wave of business transformation by unleashing the expertise of those who know best in the lines of business.
SAP Build has been designed to put SAP’s world-class enterprise technology in the hands of multidisciplinary teams in business and IT, giving them direct, secure access to the end-to-end processes, data and context they need to make smarter decisions and drive innovation quickly. SAP Build allows business experts and, in the SAP world “Key Users”, to fruitfully collaborate with IT and developers to iterate and co-create new innovations.

Last year, at SAP TechEd 2023, we announced SAP Build Code, which went generally available in March this year. Tailored for SAP development, SAP Build Code is a comprehensive, turn-key solution for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management. Developers can leverage Joule to efficiently generate application logic, data models, and test scripts. SAP Build Code enhances the entire development process, from design time to runtime, significantly boosting developers’ productivity.

And recently at SAP TechEd 2024 we achieved another milestone by adding ABAP Environments to SAP Build: with this, SAP Build is THE extensibility solution for any SAP customer: you can now accelerate your ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate processes, all in a single, comprehensive suite of AI-driven tools for extension development.

SAP Build is now the fastest growing area in SAP Business Technology Platform: we have doubled our revenue over the last year, increasing by more than 70% the active customer base. And we have had more than 200,000 active learners.

What makes SAP a visionary with SAP Build?

From my personal point of view, here are the key factors that make SAP Build the best development platform for SAP customers:

Fusion Team Development with one SAP Build offering

SAP is promoting the concept of fusion team development. Instead of converting pro-developers s to low-code developers (or vice versa), it combines its traditional developer stack with low-code SAP Build products. This multipronged development strategy resonates with enterprise developers and IT leaders who want the flexibility to use a range of approaches to build custom applications.

Fusion development is an enabler to be quicker with extending applications to your needs. What’s more, it is the foundation of allowing multidisciplinary teams to create connected solutions that fit the business’ needs, adds more people to the development and innovation equation which allows a much faster time to market, more fine-grained solutions shaped to the experts’ needs, and brings diversity into the innovation process.

Among our key innovations in Fusion Development:

  • Easy Project Discovery: Find, share and reuse application components with a centralized project lobby
  • Seamless Teamwork: Multiple developers can work simultaneously with version control and synchronized changes
  • Low-Code with Pro-Code Power: Business users can easily integrate pre-built solutions created by developers
  • Intuitive Iteration: Modify application, process & workspace designs and logic with drag-and drop ease, fostering collaboration between business and IT

Integrated Business AI capabilities

Assistance is what we expect from AI, to automate tedious tasks or to become faster with our assignments. Leveraging AI to speed up development productivity includes content recommendation sensitive to a certain context you find yourself in, allows you to naturally describe a process you’d like to create or even a data model to be created so you can focus on the creative parts of the app dev cycle, for instance, the UI creation. For that, SAP has integrated in SAP Build advanced AI capabilities that are grounded in business data: SAP Business AI understands business lingo and context. In addition, we recently launched at SAP TechEd the Joule Studio in SAP Build that will help customers and partners to build extensibility scenarios for Joule with custom skills and AI agents. So, Generative AI and Joule copilot are infused everywhere in SAP Build:

  • Application Development: Creation of business applications and automations
  • Process Automation: Process generation, process monitoring, document extraction and pre-built solution recommendations
  • Integration: Integration flow generation, pre-built integration recommendations
  • Joule Studio in SAP Build: Creating custom AI agents and skills for Joule copilot

Prebuilt LoB solutions

Content that allows developers to start with a ready-made template for their development is key for speeding up the creation process. Therefore, we are and have been investing heavily into content creation, be it ready-made APIs, process content packages, or application templates. Anything that is needed from a developer’s perspective to ease their work and make them faster in their creation process is available at hand in SAP Build available through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Both SAP and partners contribute, maintain and update pre-built content. We have now more than 500 content packages with 120+ in Finance, 115+ in Supply Chain, 40+ in Human Resources and our recent innovations in this area include, but are not limited to:

  • Finance: Capital expenditure application, automation of accrual posting and more
  • Supply Chain: Process approval for SAP IBP, Purchase requisition approval for Ariba and more
  • Human Resources: Translate job postings, position management action and more

Deep integration with business apps

SAP is unifying and standardizing its application runtimes and developer tools for numerous developer personas and use cases. This new product composition makes it easier for customers to consume SAP Build and establish partnership links. It also provides the SAP Store and SAP Business Accelerator Hub offering prebuilt reusable components that accelerate application delivery.

Recently, we have delivered:

  • Navigation from the business application UI with contextualized Extensibility Wizard to initiate an SAP Build project: available already in in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition, and soon in other SAP applications, the Extensibility Wizard is an embedded experience allowing key users to extend business applications – both on stack and side by side on SAP BTP – directly out of their business application without losing context.

For more details on how to set up the Extensibility Wizard and what extensions options are available, please read this blog post: “SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” and watch this Devtoberfest session by @Sesh_Sreenivas and @Karthik-Thiru.

  • Extensive library of connectors, integration flows and business events for SAP and non-SAP applications in SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

Learn more and get started with SAP Build!

To learn how SAP Build can optimize your finance, procurement, human resources, and more take the next step with the following resources:

  • Visit the SAP Build product page
  • If you are using S/4HANA, visit also our SAP Build for SAP S/4HANA page
  • Discover pre-built content packages on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  • Explore the basic trial and start your learning journey
  • Activate your entitlements if you’re an existing RISE and GROW with SAP customer

Use SAP Build now, not only to easily create applications, but also to automate processes and to design digital workspaces with low code and generative AI!

 

 

GARTNER is a registered trademark and service mark of Gartner and Magic Quadrant is a registered trademark of Gartner, Inc. and/or its affiliates in the U.S. and internationally and are used herein with permission. All rights reserved.

This graphic was published by Gartner, Inc. as part of a larger research document and should be evaluated in the context of the entire document. The Gartner document is available upon request from SAP.

Gartner does not endorse any vendor, product or service depicted in its research publications, and does not advise technology users to select only those vendors with the highest ratings or other designation. Gartner research publications consist of the opinions of Gartner’s research organization and should not be construed as statements of fact. Gartner disclaims all warranties, expressed or implied, with respect to this research, including any warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose.
